jokes.text: Maybe later I can get a paps mirror from an old male doctor.
jokes.setup: Liz sarcastically comments on trying on jeans being her 'favorite thing'
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd comparison with uncomfortable medical experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark observation that perfectly captures Liz's cynical worldview while addressing universal female experience with clever wordplay

jokes.text: It's huge with all the young people. I mean... all the us.
jokes.setup: Jenna tries to sound hip by mentioning 'young people'
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly corrects herself to include herself among the youth
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jenna's vanity and self-consciousness about aging

jokes.text: Because before this was a clothing store, it was a mental hospital. It's winky and fun, Liz.
jokes.setup: Liz questions why there are straightjackets everywhere
jokes.punchline: Jenna cheerfully explains the dark backstory as if it's charming
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy contrasting horrific history with trendy retail concept, perfectly executed with Jenna's oblivious enthusiasm

jokes.text: I swear to Kaballah monster.
jokes.setup: Jenna wants to emphasize her sincerity
jokes.punchline: Combines 'Kabbalah' with 'Cookie Monster' in malapropism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism that perfectly captures Jenna's shallow celebrity spirituality

jokes.text: Boy on the bottom, girl on the top.
jokes.setup: Jenna is complimenting how the jeans make Liz look
jokes.punchline: Describes the visual in accidentally sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Good character-based innuendo that works for Jenna's oblivious sexuality

jokes.text: O.M. God, those jeans are zing!
jokes.setup: Employee uses trendy abbreviation 'O.M. God'
jokes.punchline: Uses hip slang 'zing' that's about to be explained
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next joke while establishing hipster character

jokes.text: Or it's a club drug made from a tooth whitener. Either way you win.
jokes.setup: Employee explains 'zing' means amazing
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions it might also be a dangerous drug
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect casual delivery of something horrifying

jokes.text: No. Burn them. Burn them!
jokes.setup: Employee asks if she wants her old pants included
jokes.punchline: Liz dramatically demands they be destroyed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's dramatic transformation and self-loathing

jokes.text: I was attacked in my apartment...
jokes.setup: Kenneth apologizes for being late
jokes.punchline: Jack cuts him off before he can explain being attacked
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction showing Jack's obliviousness to Kenneth's suffering

jokes.text: Making political hay out of the wheat field that is the NBC-Kabletown merger
jokes.setup: Reporter describes political maneuvering
jokes.punchline: Mixes metaphors in awkward agricultural comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay mocking overwrought news language

jokes.text: What is that for? It just makes the screen go blue.
jokes.setup: Bookman criticizes Kabletown's remote control
jokes.punchline: Focuses on mundane technical complaint rather than serious issues
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about petty complaints in political rhetoric

jokes.text: It's not your fault nobody watched America's next top black guy.
jokes.setup: Jack consoles someone about diversity programming
jokes.punchline: References absurdly on-the-nose diversity show title
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate diversity efforts with perfectly absurd fake show title

jokes.text: Bookman is obviously holding a gun to my head. And no matter what I do, she obviously wants me to fail. But if she loses... This isn't a conversation!
jokes.setup: Jack appears to be talking to someone about his strategy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's talking to himself, breaks fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment showing Jack's scheming nature and self-awareness

jokes.text: I thought you said you didn't need any more friends.
jokes.setup: Jack says Austin just made a powerful friend
jokes.punchline: Jonathan references Jack's previous claim about not needing friends
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jonathan's surprising insight into Jack's contradictions

jokes.text: 'Zing leg-tubes, Liz. Vunderhinder!
jokes.setup: Kenneth commenting on Liz's jeans
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's weird slang and made-up German-sounding word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - trying to be hip but in his own weird way

jokes.text: Gay Town, White Harlem, and the Van Beardswick section of Brooklyn
jokes.setup: Liz lists BWL store locations
jokes.punchline: Each location name is an absurd gentrification parody
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of hipster gentrification with three perfect fake neighborhood names

jokes.text: These jeans totally make up for all the times I took a long, hot shower because I was bored.
jokes.setup: Liz talks about BWL's environmental consciousness
jokes.punchline: Reveals her own wasteful behavior in specific, relatable detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Liz's hypocritical environmental guilt

jokes.text: Make that all three of us. Jenna, a word. Specifically, the word 'talking.'
jokes.setup: Jenna and Liz talk about having amazing butts
jokes.punchline: Tracy includes himself then demands Jenna stop talking in clever wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that sounds like a cliche but subverts it perfectly

jokes.text: And I just learned about air quotes.
jokes.setup: Tracy discusses his Oscar chances
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions learning basic gesture as if it's significant
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Tracy's disconnect from basic social conventions

jokes.text: I talked about your movie with my therapist last week for 20 hours.
jokes.setup: Jenna claims she's fine with Tracy's success
jokes.punchline: Reveals excessive therapy time about it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jenna's jealousy while claiming the opposite

jokes.text: It's called Jenna Gets Hard.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions her exercise video
jokes.punchline: Title has unintended sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Good innuendo that fits Jenna's oblivious sexuality

jokes.text: HOFPA? - Yeah. - That's the Golden Globes. The second-most-important awards in Hollywood... After the People's Choice Awards... Where the fans are in charge.
jokes.setup: Jenna builds up importance of Golden Globes
jokes.punchline: Reveals she thinks People's Choice Awards are more important
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Jenna's warped sense of importance and celebrity

jokes.text: Be bad at snapping. Got it.
jokes.setup: Tracy asked for advice about what to do
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands complex advice
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Tracy's unique interpretation of advice

jokes.text: You're asking me?
jokes.setup: Tracy asks if bribery is wrong morally
jokes.punchline: Jenna acknowledges she's not qualified to judge morality
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware moment showing Jenna's moral flexibility

jokes.text: Actually, I go by Steve Austin. That way people see my name on ballots, they think I'm the wrestler, and they vote for me.
jokes.setup: Jack calls him Steven Austin
jokes.punchline: Reveals cynical electoral strategy based on name confusion
jokes.explanation: Good political satire showing cynical campaign tactics

jokes.text: I don't join 'em, and I never get invited to 'em...Hint, hint.
jokes.setup: Steve talks about not believing in parties
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's talking about social parties and wants an invitation
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Steve's social awkwardness

jokes.text: Goo goo gaa gaa, Jack.
jokes.setup: Steve talks about American renaissance meaning rebirth
jokes.punchline: Reduces complex political philosophy to baby sounds
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd political concept that perfectly satirizes shallow campaign themes

jokes.text: I will clean out Washington, like it's the bathroom of a paintball facility.
jokes.setup: Steve promises to clean out Washington
jokes.punchline: Uses oddly specific comparison to his actual job
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific metaphor that's both disgusting and perfectly fitting

jokes.text: And if you're blind, I am the wrestler.
jokes.setup: Campaign ad continues with standard political messaging
jokes.punchline: Adds disclaimer specifically for blind voters about wrestler confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that escalates the name confusion absurdity

jokes.text: Can I take that in two weeks?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions a 'litmus test'
jokes.punchline: Steve thinks it's literally a medical test to schedule
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Steve's literal interpretation of political metaphors

jokes.text: Dwarves, tiny buildings... Pizza bagels for lunch.
jokes.setup: Steve talks about wanting small government
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly literal interpretations of 'small'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Steve's literal interpretation with perfect absurd details

jokes.text: Or no government at all. If it works in Antarctica, why can't it work here?
jokes.setup: Talking about small government
jokes.punchline: Escalates to no government using Antarctica as positive example
jokes.explanation: Absurd political logic using the most inhospitable place as a model

jokes.text: What happens inside a man's own rain poncho at a minor league baseball game is his own business.
jokes.setup: Steve talks about government non-interference
jokes.punchline: Uses disturbing specific example
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing example that perfectly captures libertarian extremism

jokes.text: Goo goo gaa gaa.
jokes.setup: Steve demands Jack say his campaign slogan
jokes.punchline: Jack reluctantly makes baby sounds
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jack's desperation forcing him into embarrassment

jokes.text: Kenneth is our human Sushi platter
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining the Golden Globe event setup
jokes.punchline: Using Kenneth as furniture/serving ware
jokes.explanation: Perfect dehumanization of Kenneth for fancy party planning

jokes.text: Lying perfectly still reminds me of hiding under our porch during a hill people rampage.
jokes.setup: Kenneth is being used as a human sushi platter
jokes.punchline: Compares it to traumatic childhood memory matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Kenneth moment mixing his mysterious past with current absurd situation

jokes.text: I should get a chafing dish and fill it with my underwear in case some Saudi guys show up.
jokes.setup: Tracy is planning his Golden Globes event
jokes.punchline: Makes bizarre cultural assumption about foreign press
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdity showing his weird cultural misconceptions

jokes.text: You, the person who is still jealous of the attention baby Jessica got
jokes.setup: Liz confronts Jenna about helping Tracy
jokes.punchline: References Jenna's jealousy of famous baby from news story
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing the depths of Jenna's jealousy

jokes.text: I trust award shows. They tell me how much to care about different dead people.
jokes.setup: Liz says bribing award shows is awful
jokes.punchline: Reveals her cynical view of award show memorial segments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark observation about how award shows shape our grief priorities

jokes.text: sister, can you spare a breast?
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions her Lifetime original movie
jokes.punchline: Title parodies Depression-era song with breast cancer theme
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Lifetime movie absurdity

jokes.text: Just like I did to my niece when she tried to sing at our family's Christmas party.
jokes.setup: Jenna admits to sabotaging Tracy
jokes.punchline: Compares it to sabotaging child family member
jokes.explanation: Good dark character moment showing Jenna's competitive cruelty extends to family

jokes.text: Me, silly. I'm more aware of what I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Liz asks who she should be more disappointed in
jokes.punchline: Jenna argues she's worse because she's self-aware
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment showing Jenna's twisted self-awareness and pride in being awful

jokes.text: Lemon, those jeans make you look like a Mexican sports reporter.
jokes.setup: Jack sees Liz in her new jeans
jokes.punchline: Makes oddly specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Jack observation with his characteristic specific cultural reference

jokes.text: You probably know me from the future, where I am the president of the United States.
jokes.setup: Steve introduces himself to Liz
jokes.punchline: Claims she'll know him from when he's president in the future
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment showing Steve's delusional confidence

jokes.text: My name is Steve Austin, and if you're senile, yes, I am the six-million dollar man!
jokes.setup: Another campaign ad plays
jokes.punchline: Escalates wrestler confusion to include TV character for elderly voters
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the name confusion concept with perfect demographic targeting

jokes.text: I got a zappos box coming today, and there's nobody there to pick it up because my wife left me for my twin brother.
jokes.setup: Steve asks to use the phone
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly sad personal details
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation showing Steve's pathetic personal life

jokes.text: I walk around all day with a CIA tracking device in my pocket.
jokes.setup: Asked why he doesn't have a cell phone
jokes.punchline: Reveals paranoid conspiracy theory about phones
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Steve's conspiracy mindset

jokes.text: What is going on today? Has everyone lost their moral compass?
jokes.setup: Liz is confronting Jack about supporting Austin
jokes.punchline: Questions everyone's morality while wearing unethical jeans
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment that will pay off when her own hypocrisy is revealed

jokes.text: You're being awfully high and mighty for someone who once claimed her husband drowned so she could get out of a gym contract.
jokes.setup: Liz lectures Jack about morality
jokes.punchline: Jack reveals specific instance of Liz's past dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Good callback that undermines Liz's moral authority with specific example

jokes.text: Technically, I am a freelancer, which is pretty much a modern-day cowboy.
jokes.setup: Jack points out Liz works for General Electric
jokes.punchline: Liz makes absurd comparison to justify her position
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Liz's self-deceptive rationalizations

jokes.text: Also by eating beans out of a can due to impatience.
jokes.setup: Liz compares herself to a cowboy
jokes.punchline: Adds pathetic detail that undermines the cool comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-defeating escalation showing Liz's actual pathetic lifestyle

jokes.text: But hey, it's not all bad, 'cause you get to watch me walk away.
jokes.setup: Liz dramatically exits the argument
jokes.punchline: Shows off her butt in the new jeans while leaving
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing Liz's rare moment of confidence

jokes.text: Yeah, I know it's not a house, but I sleep there.
jokes.setup: Steve is on the phone about a delivery
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lives at the paintball facility
jokes.explanation: Good pathetic character detail showing Steve's living situation

jokes.text: Like the political ones where you think there'll be no boobies, then bam! Boobies.
jokes.setup: Tracy asked about foreign films that inspire him
jokes.punchline: Reduces art cinema to unexpected nudity
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment showing his simplistic view of sophisticated cinema

jokes.text: I think the better question is, what isn't an actor? A lamp. A couch. That mirror. Or a hidden pistol.
jokes.setup: Tracy asked what an actor is
jokes.punchline: Lists random objects, including suspicious 'hidden pistol'
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdity with escalation to paranoid detail

jokes.text: Now the only thing I use a football for is as a toilet.
jokes.setup: Tracy's character talks about lost dreams of football success
jokes.punchline: Reveals current degrading use of football
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy showing complete degradation of dreams

jokes.text: Funny thing to happen to a guy named 'lucky.'
jokes.setup: Tracy's character has described terrible circumstances
jokes.punchline: Reveals his ironic nickname
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between name and circumstances

jokes.text: Hand me that shovel so I can dig a grave for her.
jokes.setup: Steve practicing campaign rhetoric about forceful conclusion
jokes.punchline: Escalates to violent metaphor about opponent
jokes.explanation: Good dark political rhetoric that goes too far

jokes.text: My generation never votes. It interferes with talking about ourselves all the time.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bookman courting youth vote
jokes.punchline: Liz explains why young people don't vote
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social observation that perfectly captures millennial self-absorption

jokes.text: Brooklyn Zack. He throws pool parties in dumpsters.
jokes.setup: Liz defends BWL ownership
jokes.punchline: Describes owner with absurd hipster behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of trying-too-hard hipster authenticity

jokes.text: they had sweatshops make them into messenger bags to sell to outer-borough idiots.
jokes.setup: Jack explains Halliburton's surplus waterboarding hoods
jokes.punchline: Reveals they became trendy accessories for Brooklyn hipsters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark satire connecting torture devices to hipster fashion consumption

jokes.text: It's pronounced hond-made in oosa. The hond people are a vietnamese slave tribe, and USA is their island prison.
jokes.setup: Liz points to 'Handmade in USA' label
jokes.punchline: Jack reveals horrific truth behind the label through pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant wordplay that creates an entire dark mythology from mispronunciation

jokes.text: You know how they get the stitching so small? Orphans.
jokes.setup: Jack is explaining the jeans' production
jokes.punchline: Reveals child labor in matter-of-fact way
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivery of horrific information with casual tone

jokes.text: Brooklyn Zack is real. He just got back from Peru, where he met a family that's been making hats for 2,000 years.
jokes.setup: Liz refuses to believe Jack's revelation
jokes.punchline: Defends with stereotypical artisan origin story
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's desperate denial with cliched justification

jokes.text: The New York times is owned by NYT incorporated, which is owned by Altheon Ballistic Dynamics, which is owned by the Murdoch family, who are owned... By Halliburton.
jokes.setup: Liz says liberal media would have reported BWL truth
jokes.punchline: Jack traces media ownership back to Halliburton in escalating chain
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation building to absurd conspiracy theory conclusion

jokes.text: Halliburton, bitch. So what?
jokes.setup: Jack reveals NBC is owned by Halliburton
jokes.punchline: Jonathan casually dismisses it with attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion showing Jonathan's unexpected attitude

jokes.text: Oh, so gay men can't be conservative? You're so 'norant. That's short for ignorant.
jokes.setup: Liz questions how Jonathan can work there
jokes.punchline: Jonathan defends himself with made-up abbreviation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with clever wordplay showing Jonathan's unexpected political views

jokes.text: His great grandfather was Domingo Halliburton.
jokes.setup: Jack points to Che Guevara poster
jokes.punchline: Reveals even revolutionary icon has Halliburton connection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of conspiracy showing even revolutionary icons are connected

jokes.text: Perlman, isn't that last name Jew...
jokes.setup: Jack introduces Steve to Ed Perlman
jokes.punchline: Steve starts to make antisemitic comment
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing Steve's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Maybe I'll add to it by making some mysterious sounds. - Oooooohhh... Yeeee-eeee... - I love it.
jokes.setup: Jack suggests Steve cultivate an aura of mystery
jokes.punchline: Steve immediately makes ridiculous ghost sounds
jokes.explanation: Good absurd character moment with Jack's desperate approval

jokes.text: Your mother exploded.
jokes.setup: Emotional family dinner scene in Tracy's movie
jokes.punchline: Casually devastating revelation delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant dark comedy line that's both shocking and absurd

jokes.text: That movie gave me drunk-in-the-bathtub face.
jokes.setup: Jenna watches Tracy's emotional movie
jokes.punchline: Describes crying in oddly specific way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna observation with specific self-referential description

jokes.text: Like Anne Coulter's underwear.
jokes.setup: Liz describes BWL as crunchy outside, right-wing inside
jokes.punchline: Makes comparison to conservative pundit's undergarments
jokes.explanation: Good political comparison with disturbing imagery

jokes.text: ♪ back it up, back it up ♪ ♪ and drop it like it's hot ♪ ♪ drop it like it's hot ♪ ♪ I will haunt your dreams ♪
jokes.setup: Liz describes wanting to do a dance move for Carol
jokes.punchline: Song lyrics escalate to threatening
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from innocent dance to threatening with perfect Liz delivery

jokes.text: I believe in Steve Austin and his plan to put a casino on the moon.
jokes.setup: Liz defends her support for Austin
jokes.punchline: Mentions his absurd policy position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd policy position that perfectly fits Steve's character

jokes.text: Like waking up next to Rob Schneider furious.
jokes.setup: Jenna describes how furious she'll be when Tracy wins
jokes.punchline: Uses oddly specific comparison to sleeping with unfunny comedian
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna comparison with specific Hollywood reference and implied promiscuity

jokes.text: Compromises are for lesser souls. Die, werewolf zombie!
jokes.setup: Tracy quotes from another of his movies about compromises
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with action movie line
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of philosophical wisdom with ridiculous action dialogue

jokes.text: I would hit that. - Too small.
jokes.setup: Characters commenting on Liz walking away in tight jeans
jokes.punchline: Second person critiques her butt size
jokes.explanation: Functional visual gag callback to earlier jean emphasis but relatively weak

jokes.text: Lesbian Mario brothers.
jokes.setup: Steve giving his campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Random nonsensical phrase in political context
jokes.explanation: Random absurdism that fits Steve's character but lacks structure

jokes.text: And I'm including 2008's turtleneck with smiley-face vest.
jokes.setup: Jack says Liz's shorteralls are the most unflattering clothing ever
jokes.punchline: References specific past fashion disaster with absurd detail
jokes.explanation: Good callback with specific absurd fashion combination that sounds perfectly Liz

jokes.text: because in my shorteralls I found a bag of tastetations, a discontinued chocolate hard candy
jokes.setup: Liz explains why she feels good
jokes.punchline: Found candy with made-up name in her pocket
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with invented product name that sounds plausible

jokes.text: I will never allow casinos on the moon!
jokes.setup: Steve sees Liz in shorteralls
jokes.punchline: Dramatically reverses his moon casino position
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Steve's policy positions are based on whatever he sees

jokes.text: ♪ Ooga booga big ♪ ♪ ooga booga strong ♪ ♪ I am going to sing my ooga booga song ♪ ♪ ooga booga big ♪ ♪ ooga booga strong ♪
jokes.setup: Steve announces he's written a new national anthem
jokes.punchline: Song is completely nonsensical baby talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of baby theme into actual campaign anthem with perfect absurdity

jokes.text: That's worse than the speech my grandpa made when my cousin married a Japanese girl.
jokes.setup: Jack reacts to Steve's terrible anthem performance
jokes.punchline: Compares it to racist family speech
jokes.explanation: Good dark character moment revealing Jack's family racism history

jokes.text: Cake boy!
jokes.setup: Jack wants cake to cheer up
jokes.punchline: Calls Kenneth like summoning a servant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's imperious treatment of Kenneth

jokes.text: We should bring back slavery!
jokes.setup: Montage of Steve's campaign positions
jokes.punchline: Extreme racist policy position
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation of Steve's extremism but relies on shock value

jokes.text: As God is my witness, we will build casinos on the moon!
jokes.setup: Final montage of Steve's positions
jokes.punchline: Returns to moon casino policy with dramatic emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good callback ending with dramatic delivery of absurd campaign promise
